questions.question: The test scores of 7 students are: 72, 85, 90, 85, 78, 92, 85. What is the median score?
questions.explanation: Order the data: 72, 78, 85, 85, 85, 90, 92. With 7 values, the median is the 4th value: 85.

questions.question: The daily high temperatures (in °F) for a week: 58, 62, 59, 65, 58, 61, 64. What is the mean? Round to the nearest tenth.
The ages of 9 employees are: 22, 25, 28, 30, 32, 35, 38, 40, 55. What is the interquartile range (IQR)?
A box plot has minimum = 10, Q1 = 18, median = 25, Q3 = 34, maximum = 50. What is the range of the data?
A box plot has minimum = 10, Q1 = 18, median = 25, Q3 = 34, maximum = 50. What percentage of the data lies between 18 and 34?
questions.explanation: The box in a box plot spans from Q1 to Q3, which contains the middle 50% of the data.

questions.question: A line of best fit for a scatter plot is $y = 2.5x + 30$, where $x$ is hours studied and $y$ is test score. Predict the test score for a student who studied 8 hours.

The table shows cafeteria preferences. What percentage of students who prefer burgers are girls? Round to the nearest percent.

$$\begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|}\hline & \text{Pizza} & \text{Burgers} & \text{Total} \\ \hline \text{Boys} & 24 & 16 & 40 \\ \hline \text{Girls} & 30 & 10 & 40 \\ \hline \text{Total} & 54 & 26 & 80 \\ \hline\end{array}$$
questions.explanation: Girls who prefer burgers = 10. Total burger = 26. Percentage = (10/26) × 100 ≈ 38.46%, rounds to 38%.

questions.question:
The table shows transportation by grade. Given that a student rides the bus, what is the conditional relative frequency that they are in 9th grade? Express as a simplified fraction.

$$\begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|}\hline & \text{Car} & \text{Bus} & \text{Total} \\ \hline \text{9th} & 20 & 30 & 50 \\ \hline \text{10th} & 35 & 15 & 50 \\ \hline \text{Total} & 55 & 45 & 100 \\ \hline\end{array}$$
questions.explanation: 9th graders on bus = 30. Total bus riders = 45. Fraction = 30/45 = 2/3.

questions.question: A survey of 200 randomly selected students found that 140 prefer online learning. The school has 1,500 students. Estimate how many students in the entire school prefer online learning.
Hours spent on homework per week: 2, 3, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, 15. What is the mean absolute deviation (MAD)? Round to the nearest tenth.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have a strong grasp of data analysis concepts including measures of center, spread, box plots, two-way tables, and making inferences from data.
scoreMessages.70: Good work. Review: IQR = Q3 - Q1. For conditional relative frequency, the denominator is the row or column total, not the grand total. MAD is the average of absolute deviations from the mean.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing. Focus on: 1) Ordering data before finding median and quartiles, 2) Using the correct denominator for conditional frequencies, 3) Setting up proportions for data inferences.
scoreMessages.0: Start by: 1) Ordering data for median, Q1, Q3, and IQR, 2) Computing mean by summing and dividing, 3) Reading two-way tables carefully to find joint and marginal frequencies, 4) Using proportions to make predictions about larger populations.
